old_claim.note: Natural Dyes provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The workshop's yarn-production stage is evidenced by 47 spindle whorls of varied forms and sizes.

source_shape.source_locator: Textile tools section and Table 1 report 47 spindle whorls from room 28 and reconstruct spinning and yarn production from them.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Room 28 contains a large spindle-whorl assemblage alongside loom weights and other equipment in a probable textile workshop.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Spinning is an evidenced production stage in this workshop, while dyeing is not required to produce the natural tabby textile or operate the room.

ontology_before: A general household workshop required dyeing but omitted its directly evidenced spinning equipment.
ontology_after: The Beycesultan workshop requires spindle-based yarn production and does not require an optional dye process.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: household_textile_production now requires drop_spindles and no longer depends on natural_dyes.
would_reject_if: The room 28 perforated objects were shown not to be spindle whorls or not to belong to the workshop context.
short_reason: Spindle whorls are direct workshop evidence; dyeing is not constitutive of the scoped room.
